title: The critique prompt

spec.title: The critique prompt
spec.notes_for_generator: Ask AI to critique its own output. Introduce self-evaluation — getting the AI to find weaknesses in what it just produced. This is a harder mechanic that requires the learner to have gotten a first output and then ask specifically for critique. Hinted: mention that AI can evaluate its own work and that being specific about what you want it to look for matters.

content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 9: Make the model critique itself

Most people accept the first thing an LLM gives them. That's leaving work on the table — and it's not even hard to fix.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
───────────────
✂️ EDITING | 🔵 STEADY STATE | ⏱ 15–20 min

THE BRIEF:
You've drafted a short product pitch — maybe for an internal initiative, a new feature, or an idea you want to get buy-in on. You've already used an LLM to help shape or write it. Now you want to know: where is this actually weak? You're not looking for flattery. You want the genuine gaps before someone else finds them.

YOUR REPS:
1. Ask the AI to produce a short product pitch for something real — an initiative, a feature, a product idea you're actually working on or interested in. It should be 150–250 words, aimed at a decision-maker who'll be sceptical. Keep it in your back pocket: you'll be critiquing it next.

2. Here's the hint for today: An LLM can critique its own output — but only if you give it specific criteria, and only as a second opinion rather than a final verdict. Don't ask "is this good?" That gets you reassurance. Instead, ask it to critique the pitch it just wrote against concrete criteria: clarity of the problem being solved, strength of the evidence or rationale, whether the ask is specific enough, and any assumptions it's made that a sceptic would challenge. See what it surfaces.

3. Take one specific weakness the AI identified and ask it to revise only that part. Then compare the before and after side by side. Did the revision actually fix the problem — or just move words around? If it moved words around, tell it exactly what you wanted and try again.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
• The critique in rep 2 identifies at least one weakness that genuinely surprised you or that you hadn't spotted yourself
• The weaknesses named are specific (e.g. "the problem statement assumes the reader already cares about X") — not vague ("could be clearer")
• The revision in rep 3 makes a substantive change, not a cosmetic one — the argument is stronger, not just the wording

💡 COACHING NOTE:
Asking an LLM to critique its own output is one of the most underused moves in the toolkit. It is not an independent reviewer, and it can miss its own mistakes, but it has no emotional attachment to the draft — so a well-framed critique prompt can surface weaknesses you would otherwise skip. The key is specificity: "critique this" produces generalities, while "find the assumptions a sceptic would challenge" produces something you can actually use. This principle transfers everywhere — to reviewing your own writing, to giving feedback on others' work, to stress-testing a plan. The quality of the question determines the quality of the critical eye.

──────────
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive.
Share the programme: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
Found this useful? You can leave a tip: https://ko-fi.com/theaihabit
review_notes: Clarified that AI Habit focuses on chat-based large language models (LLMs), while using AI as public shorthand.
Kept exercises model-agnostic and usable with any text-based LLM or approved work tool.
Reworked research/reasoning language to avoid over-trusting model confidence or hidden chain-of-thought.
Reframed AI Habit as a grounded free 14-day starter block with a paid continuation for deeper 60-90 day habit-building, rather than a validation experiment.

Positioned the paid continuation as The 90-Day AI Habit: a structured applied-LLM programme covering writing/editing, strategy/decision-making, research/assessment, management/delegation, personal productivity, and practical outputs.
Simplified the 90-day commercial route: join individually, bring a small group for a discounted group subscription, or use the workplace launch pack to get an employer to pay.
Added ethical upgrade nudges to Days 3, 7, and 10: endowed progress, career identity, and momentum protection for The 90-Day AI Habit.
Mechanical repair pass: normalised workout badge line after scheduler verification.
